title: Review the post-change documentation risk in /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public: which user/operator-facing docs now contradict the new default assist=none path? Prioritize anything that could mislead operations or make tests/checks fail. Do not edit files; return concise findings with fi...
summary_text: Recommend **(a): archive externally, then remove generated artifacts only**. Why: - **Operator safety:** it avoids hiding real tracked edits in a stash (which can be forgotten, dropped, or conflict on pop). - **Recovery simplicity:** meaningful changes stay visible in place; only regenerable files are cleaned. - **Lower risk surface:** stash-everything is stateful and error-prone in large dirty trees. If you need a truly pristine tree for a task, safest is a **separate temp worktree/clone**, not stashing the main dirty tree.
